summary: Possible namespace confusion with redis-om-node; unusual transitive dependency
details: Package name 'redis-type-os' does not correspond to its declared repository 'github:redis/redis-om-node', and dist/index.js is a near-verbatim copy of the redis-om compiled output (same exports, same lib paths). The package.json also declares a dependency on 'void-ulid' (^3.0.2) rather than the well-known 'ulid' library. No install lifecycle hooks, no network exfiltration, no obfuscation, and no credential or environment reads were observed in the reachable code. Without an installer-harm payload in this package itself, the name/metadata mismatch and the unusual ulid substitution are inconclusive on their own — they could indicate a confusion-style squat, an unofficial fork, or an abandoned mirror. The 'void-ulid' transitive should also be assessed separately, as it is the more likely vehicle for any harm in this dependency chain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
